_note: Round-8 follow-up #4: a typed dispatcher with a TYPE-REFERENCE arg (`createEventDispatcher<MyEvents>()`) shouldn't disqualify the runes fn-component shape. Upstream's `events.hasEvents()` (ComponentEvents.ts:231) only counts events from typed dispatchers when the type arg is an INLINE type literal — its `members` list is enumerable at parse time and each PropertySignature feeds `addToEvents`. A type reference to an alias has no member list (the alias resolution happens at type-check time, not at the AST level), so events.size stays 0 and events.hasEvents() is false → upstream picks fn-shape. Pre-fix native gated on `synthesized_events_type.is_some()` which fires for ANY typed dispatcher (literal OR ref) and pushed this Producer onto the iso `$$IsomorphicComponent` shape; Consumer's `(typeof Producer)[]` then fired TS2322 (callable-only function expr can't satisfy the iso shape's `new(...)` ctor). Post-fix native exposes a precise `has_inline_typed_dispatcher_members` signal in svn-analyze and ORs it with `synthesized_untyped_events.is_some()` for the fn-shape gate; type-ref-only typed dispatchers no longer disqualify.
